allies.rationale_text: Critical-thinking helps survivorship-bias resist vivid winner stories by demanding the missing evidence and the hidden denominator before trusting the lesson.
allies.source_quote: Critical thinking is the ability to notice biases, question your own ideas, and evaluate evidence objectively.

allies.rationale_text: Premortem strengthens survivorship-bias by forcing the team to imagine failure cases that winner-only evidence would otherwise hide.
allies.source_quote: non-surviving data points to truly stress-test edge cases

allies.rationale_text: Base-rates are the direct antidote to survivorship-bias because they restore the broader outcome distribution that vivid survivor stories erase.
allies.source_quote: outside view demands that one explicitly looks at the probabilities of the wider class of outcomes

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias compounds survivorship-bias by locking attention onto the successful cases that confirm the favored theory while failures disappear from view.
antagonists.source_quote: confirmation Bias can exacerbate Survivorship Bias by reinforcing focus on successful outcomes, ignoring failures

antagonists.rationale_text: Optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy intensifies survivorship-bias by making the winner story feel like a likely template for success instead of a filtered special case.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Survivorship-bias conflicts with confirmation-bias when focus on successful outcomes is reinforced while failures are ignored.
structured_tensions.source_quote: survivorship-bias vs confirmation-bias: survivorship-bias conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when confirmation Bias can exacerbate Survivorship Bias by reinforcing focus on successful outcomes, ignoring failures.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Survivorship-bias conflicts with optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy when overestimating success likelihood overwhelms the caution implied by missing failures.
structured_tensions.source_quote: survivorship-bias vs optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy: survivorship-bias conflicts with optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy (optimism bias and planning fallacy) when optimism Bias and Planning Fallacy may lead to overestimating success likelihood, conflicting with the caution urged by Survivorship Bias.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on denominator recovery through critical and base-rate allies plus explicit conflicts with confirmation and optimism distortion, so the file preserves that conservative surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation widening beyond the clearly named outside-view and anti-success-story neighborhood.
open_questions: Team processes are source-real allies too, but the retained set favors cleaner first-order current-vocabulary mappings.
